title: Clean Code Principles for Frontend Developers

questions.sentence: Writing code that works is only the first half of a developer's job; the second half is writing code that remains maintainable.
'Clean Code' is a set of principles that focus on readability and simplicity.
One of the most important rules is choosing meaningful names for variables and functions.
A good function name should also be a verb that describes exactly what it does.
If a function is too long, it should be broken down into smaller, specialized helper functions.
By following these clean code practices, you make it much easier for yourself and your teammates to understand and debug the code in the future.
